pages.raw_text:
The long fight of the James
Smyth Plumbing company and the
Plumbers union Of this city, which
threatened to depose Ethan Allen,
city plumbing inspector, from office,
was brought to a close Saturday
evening. A committee of from
the union met Smyth and agreed to
take back into the union, without
fine, all the men who had left it
at the beginning of the trouble; to
admit to the union of this city all
the men who had been brought
from Portland to work in Smyth's
open shop, and to eliminate the
limit clause from all plumbing
contracts. The trouble started last
fall over special plumbing per-
NIT issued by City Plumbing In
spector Ethan Allen to Smyth, for
the plumbing of the Columbia
building, corner First av. and How
ard ST
